nodes.name: VM Consumer
nodes.description: Application or platform-engineer user who declares VirtualMachine custom resources via kubectl / GitOps. Never interacts with the underlying hypervisor SDKs directly — that is the whole point of banlieue.

nodes.name: Platform Operator
nodes.description: Cluster administrator who installs the banlieue CRDs, deploys the controller and provider controllers, and configures Provider CRs with backend credentials.

nodes.name: Management Kubernetes Cluster
nodes.description: Kubernetes cluster (>=1.27) that hosts the banlieue controller and every provider controller. Acts as the control plane for VM lifecycle management across heterogeneous hypervisor backends.

nodes.name: Kubernetes API Server
nodes.description: Management cluster API server. The ONLY communication bus between the banlieue controller and every provider controller. Persists VirtualMachine, VMClass, VMImage, Provider, and infrastructure CRs; mediates every state transition.

nodes.name: banlieue-controller
nodes.description: Main Rust kube-rs controller. Watches VirtualMachine CRs, resolves class/image/providerRef references, creates a backend-specific infrastructure CR carrying the resolved spec, and mirrors status from that infra CR back onto the VirtualMachine. Owns no backend credentials — all backend access is delegated to provider controllers via CRDs.

crd-only-contract.description: Controller MUST NOT speak RPC, REST, gRPC, or any out-of-band protocol to provider controllers. All inter-controller communication is via Kubernetes CRDs and the API server. This is a project non-negotiable (see CLAUDE.md "Non-Negotiables" #1).

least-privilege.description: Controller ServiceAccount holds a ClusterRole scoped to banlieue.io/* and infrastructure.banlieue.io/* CRDs plus events and Secrets (read for resolving Provider.credentialsRef). No cluster-admin.

nodes.name: banlieue-provider-vsphere (planned, Phase 1B)
nodes.description: Provider controller that watches VSphereMachine / VSphereMachineTemplate infrastructure CRs and drives vCenter via the govmomi-equivalent Rust client. Satisfies the CAPI v1beta2 InfraMachine contract so the same provider can also serve CAPI Clusters. Status: PLANNED — Phase 1B in the roadmap; no code in-tree as of this revision.

nodes.name: banlieue-provider-proxmox (planned, Phase 1C)
nodes.description: Provider controller for Proxmox VE clusters. Watches ProxmoxMachine infrastructure CRs and drives the Proxmox HTTP API. Status: PLANNED — Phase 1C.

nodes.name: banlieue-provider-libvirt (planned, Phase 1D)
nodes.description: Provider controller for libvirt/KVM hosts. Watches LibvirtMachine infrastructure CRs and drives libvirt over TCP/TLS or SSH. Status: PLANNED — Phase 1D.

nodes.name: banlieue (single binary)
nodes.description: One Rust executable that packages every controller role. Dispatches subcommands: `banlieue controller` runs the main controller, `banlieue provider <name>` (e.g. `banlieue provider vsphere`) runs a backend provider. Each role lives in an independent library crate with its own dependency graph; providers are gated behind per-provider Cargo features (default = all). One container image ships this binary; Deployments select the role via container args. See ADR-0004.

nodes.name: vSphere / vCenter Backend
nodes.description: Real VMware vSphere environment (vCenter + ESXi hosts) reached by the vSphere provider over HTTPS. The address, credentials, and datacenter are declared in a Provider CR.

nodes.name: Proxmox VE Backend
nodes.description: Real Proxmox VE cluster reached by the Proxmox provider over its HTTPS API on port 8006.

nodes.name: libvirt / KVM Host
nodes.description: libvirt/KVM host reached by the libvirt provider over libvirt's native TCP/TLS protocol (port 16514) or SSH transport.

nodes.name: VirtualMachine Custom Resource
nodes.description: Namespaced CRD (banlieue.io/v1alpha1) declaring a backend-agnostic VM: class (→ VMClass), image (→ VMImage), providerRef (→ Provider), and optional userData. The ONLY user-facing API.

nodes.name: Provider Custom Resource
nodes.description: Namespaced CRD (banlieue.io/v1alpha1) declaring the existence of a backend in this cluster: type (vsphere|proxmox|libvirt), endpoint, datacenter, credentialsRef to a Secret. Owned by the platform operator, referenced by VirtualMachine.

nodes.name: VMClass Custom Resource
nodes.description: Cluster-scoped CRD (banlieue.io/v1alpha1) declaring a flavour: CPU count, memory, disk shape. Referenced by VirtualMachine.spec.class by name.

nodes.name: VMImage Custom Resource
nodes.description: Cluster-scoped CRD (banlieue.io/v1alpha1) declaring a boot image and its source. Referenced by VirtualMachine.spec.image by name; resolved by the provider against its backend's native image catalog.

nodes.name: Infrastructure Machine CR (VSphereMachine, etc.)
nodes.description: Namespaced CRD (infrastructure.banlieue.io/v1alpha1) created by the banlieue-controller as the message to the provider. Satisfies the CAPI v1beta2 InfraMachine contract so the same provider can serve both banlieue and CAPI. Status is owned by the provider; the banlieue controller mirrors it back onto the VirtualMachine.

nodes.name: Infrastructure Cluster CR (VSphereCluster)
nodes.description: Namespaced CRD (infrastructure.banlieue.io/v1alpha1) satisfying the CAPI v1beta2 InfraCluster contract. A CAPI Cluster.spec.infrastructureRef points at it. The banlieue-controller reconciles it by aggregating status.failureDomains[] from one or more Provider CRs into the CAPI v1beta2 failure-domain list — so one Kubernetes cluster can span multiple vCenters. No backend access: pure CRD aggregation of Provider status. See ADR-0002.

nodes.name: CAPI Cluster / MachineDeployment (external)
nodes.description: Upstream Cluster API objects (cluster.x-k8s.io): Cluster, MachineDeployment, and a control-plane object. NOT owned by banlieue — owned by CAPI core and a control-plane provider (k0smotron for k0s). 'replicas: N' on these objects, spread across the InfraCluster's failure domains, is how banlieue provisions a Kubernetes cluster (ADR-0001). banlieue is CAPI's infrastructure provider, not its cluster controller.

nodes.name: Cluster API core + control-plane provider (k0smotron)
nodes.description: Upstream CAPI controllers (cluster, machineset, machinedeployment) plus a control-plane/bootstrap provider — k0smotron for k0s. Reads VSphereCluster.status.failureDomains, balances Machines across them (count-based round-robin), and mints VSphereMachines from a VSphereMachineTemplate. banlieue does NOT ship these; the platform operator installs them. This is what lets any CAPI consumer (kubeadm, RKE2, k0smotron) use banlieue as an infra provider.

relationships.description: The single `banlieue` binary is composed of every controller role. Each role is invoked as a subcommand (`banlieue controller`, `banlieue provider vsphere`, …) and lives in an independent library crate; the binary only dispatches. See ADR-0004.
The management cluster hosts the banlieue controller, every provider controller, and the Kubernetes API server itself.

Every banlieue CR (VirtualMachine, Provider, VMClass, VMImage, infrastructure machines) is persisted by the management-cluster API server in etcd. The API server is therefore the bus AND the source of truth for state.
VM consumer authors and applies VirtualMachine CRs (and references VMClass / VMImage / Provider by name).
Platform operator authors Provider CRs and the backing Secret (out of band of consumer flow). Consumers never see provider credentials.

Platform operator authors the CAPI Cluster, control-plane object, MachineDeployment(s), the VSphereCluster (InfraCluster), and a VSphereMachineTemplate to provision a Kubernetes (k0s) cluster. 'replicas: N' on the CAPI objects drives failure-domain spread.
VM consumer applies manifests via kubectl / GitOps to the management-cluster Kubernetes API.
Platform operator applies CRDs, Provider CRs, and the controller deployment via kubectl / GitOps.

banlieue-controller watches VirtualMachine / Provider / VMClass / VMImage / VSphereCluster CRs, server-side-applies infrastructure machine CRs, watches their status, and patches VirtualMachine status with the mirrored conditions. It also reconciles VSphereCluster (InfraCluster) by aggregating selected Providers' status.failureDomains[] into the CAPI v1beta2 failure-domain list — reading Provider status only, no backend access.
vSphere provider controller watches VSphereMachine infrastructure CRs and patches their .status. NEVER speaks to the banlieue controller directly — the K8s API is the bus.
Proxmox provider controller watches ProxmoxMachine infrastructure CRs and patches their .status. Same CRD-only contract as the vSphere provider.
libvirt provider controller watches LibvirtMachine infrastructure CRs and patches their .status. Same CRD-only contract.

CAPI core + control-plane provider (k0smotron) watch Cluster / MachineDeployment / VSphereCluster, read VSphereCluster.status.failureDomains, and create Machine + VSphereMachine objects spread across those failure domains. Pure Kubernetes API — banlieue never talks to CAPI out of band.
vSphere provider talks to vCenter over HTTPS to create, query, update, and destroy VMs.

tls-trust-byoc.description: The vSphere provider owns the HTTP transport (Bring-Your-Own-Client): it builds the reqwest::Client and injects it via vim_rs ClientBuilder::http_client, rather than letting vim_rs construct one. banlieue therefore controls TLS trust establishment end to end — honouring Provider.spec.connection.caBundle (a value-or-source: inline PEM, or a configMapRef / secretRef key defaulting to ca.crt; exactly one, enforced controller-side and by a ValidatingAdmissionPolicy per ADR-0007), added as root certificate(s); insecureSkipTLSVerify is a separate, explicit opt-out. Resolving a configMapRef requires the provider ClusterRole to read configmaps (get/list/watch), alongside the existing secrets read. The transport is OpenSSL-free: vim_rs 0.5 (reqwest 0.13/rustls) is consumed in BYOC mode and the reqwest client uses the rustls ring provider shared with kube, installed once as the process default (ADR-0009). See ADR-0008 (BYOC runtime design) and ADR-0009 (0.5 migration + build end state).

relationships.description: Proxmox provider talks to Proxmox VE over its HTTPS API on port 8006.
libvirt provider talks to a libvirtd over native TCP/TLS (port 16514) or SSH transport.

flows.name: Create a VirtualMachine
flows.description: User applies a VirtualMachine CR; the banlieue controller resolves its references and creates a backend-specific infrastructure CR; the matching provider controller observes the infra CR, provisions on its backend, and patches status; the banlieue controller mirrors the status back onto the VirtualMachine.

transitions.description: VM consumer kubectl-applies a VirtualMachine CR with class, image, and providerRef. Persisted in etcd via the management-cluster API server.
banlieue-controller watch fires. Controller resolves the named VMClass, VMImage, and Provider; computes the desired infrastructure spec.

banlieue-controller server-side-applies a VSphereMachine (or ProxmoxMachine / LibvirtMachine) CR with the resolved spec, ownerReference back to the VirtualMachine, and the controller's field manager 'banlieue.io/controller'.
The matching provider controller's watch fires on the new infrastructure CR. Provider reads its spec.
Provider talks to its backend (vCenter / Proxmox / libvirt) to create the underlying VM, attach disks, attach network, power on.
Provider patches infrastructure-CR .status with CAPI v1beta2 conditions (Ready, addresses[], providerID, etc.) using its own field manager (e.g. 'banlieue.io/provider-vsphere').
banlieue-controller watch on infrastructure CRs fires. Controller mirrors the status (Ready, conditions, addresses) onto VirtualMachine.status — never sets Ready=true on its own.

no-rpc-between-controllers.description: Every transition in this flow is a Kubernetes API call. There is no direct controller-to-controller communication. Enforces the CRD-only contract.

flows.name: Swap a VirtualMachine's backend (least-touch)
flows.description: User changes a single field — VirtualMachine.spec.providerRef.name — to repoint a VM from one backend to another. The banlieue controller tears down the old infrastructure CR and creates a new one of the right kind on the new provider. The user's manifest does not otherwise change. This is the canonical demonstration of banlieue's abstraction principle.

transitions.description: VM consumer patches VirtualMachine.spec.providerRef.name (e.g. 'prod-vsphere' → 'prod-proxmox').
banlieue-controller observes the spec change. Detects that the existing infrastructure CR no longer matches the desired provider type.
Controller deletes the stale VSphereMachine CR; the vSphere provider observes the deletion and tears down the backend VM via finalizer; ownerReference cascade clears the infra CR.
Controller server-side-applies a ProxmoxMachine CR with the same uniform spec. The Proxmox provider observes the new CR and provisions on Proxmox VE.
Provider patches new infra-CR status; controller mirrors Ready=true back onto the VirtualMachine. Consumer's manifest still looks identical except for one field.

least-touch.description: Demonstrates banlieue's user-facing invariant: changing the backend MUST NOT require changes outside spec.providerRef.name. Any feature that breaks this is rejected.

flows.name: Delete a VirtualMachine (finalizer cleanup)
flows.description: Deletion is gated by two finalizers (controller, provider) to guarantee that the backend VM is torn down BEFORE the CR is removed from etcd. No leaks.

transitions.description: VM consumer kubectl-deletes the VirtualMachine. API server sets deletionTimestamp; CR persists because the controller finalizer 'banlieue.io/virtualmachine' is still attached.
banlieue-controller observes deletionTimestamp. Issues a delete on the owned infrastructure machine CR.
Provider observes deletionTimestamp on its infra CR (its own finalizer is attached). Begins backend teardown.
Provider talks to the backend (vCenter / Proxmox / libvirt) to power-off, detach storage, and delete the underlying VM. Confirms via backend API.
Provider clears its finalizer; infra CR is garbage-collected by the API server.
banlieue-controller observes the infra CR disappear; clears its own finalizer on the VirtualMachine; CR is garbage-collected. No backend leak.

finalizer-ordering.description: Two-level finalizer chain (controller-side waits for provider-side to clear) guarantees the backend VM is destroyed before the CR is. A crash anywhere in the chain is replay-safe because reconciliation is idempotent.

flows.name: Provision a Kubernetes cluster via CAPI (banlieue as infra provider)
flows.description: The platform operator provisions a k0s cluster by applying upstream CAPI objects plus banlieue's VSphereCluster (InfraCluster) and a VSphereMachineTemplate. banlieue does NOT own cluster lifecycle — CAPI + a control-plane provider (k0smotron) do. banlieue's job is to advertise failure domains (VSphereCluster) and realise each machine (VSphereMachine). 'replicas: N' spread across failure domains is how a tier (e.g. 6/6 'platinum') is expressed (ADR-0001).

transitions.description: Operator applies a CAPI Cluster (infrastructureRef → VSphereCluster), a control-plane object, MachineDeployment(s) with replica counts, the VSphereCluster, and a VSphereMachineTemplate.

banlieue-controller's VSphereCluster watch fires. It resolves the selected Provider CRs (providerRefs / providerSelector), aggregates their status.failureDomains[] into VSphereCluster.status.failureDomains (CAPI v1beta2 list), applies controlPlaneFailureDomainSelector, and sets initialization.provisioned + Ready. No vCenter access.

CAPI core + control-plane provider read VSphereCluster.status.failureDomains and balance the requested replicas across them (count-based round-robin), creating one Machine + one VSphereMachine per placement from the VSphereMachineTemplate, each stamped with spec.failureDomain.

The vSphere provider's VSphereMachine watch fires for each new machine (the existing Create-a-VirtualMachine machine path). It maps the failure domain to a (datacenter, cluster) and reads the resolved spec.
Provider clones the VM into the chosen compute cluster's resource pool and powers it on. vSphere DRS selects the ESXi host within that cluster — banlieue does not pick the host.

Provider patches VSphereMachine.status (provisioned, providerID, addresses); CAPI mirrors it up to the Machine, and the control-plane provider (k0smotron) joins the nodes into the k0s cluster once the control plane is reachable.

capi-native-failure-domain-spread.description: Cluster-level replica management and failure-domain spread are delegated to CAPI; banlieue only advertises failure domains (InfraCluster) and realises machines (InfraMachine). banlieue ships NO native cluster/tier controller. This keeps banlieue reusable by any CAPI consumer and avoids duplicating MachineSet/control-plane logic (ADR-0001).

code-first-crds.description: All CRDs are generated from the Rust type system in crates/banlieue-api via the crdgen binary; YAML in deploy/crds/ is never hand-edited. Prevents drift between Rust types and the schemas the API server validates against.

capi-v1beta2-infra-machine-contract.description: Every banlieue provider's infrastructure CRD satisfies the upstream Cluster API v1beta2 InfraMachine contract. Buys a battle-tested status model AND lets the same provider also serve CAPI Clusters without modification. CAPI discovers compliance via the CRD-level label cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta2=v1alpha1, emitted onto every infrastructure.banlieue.io CRD by crdgen post-processing (ADR-0005) since kube-derive cannot set CRD labels.

capi-v1beta2-infra-cluster-contract.description: banlieue's InfraCluster CRD (VSphereCluster) satisfies the upstream Cluster API v1beta2 InfraCluster contract: status.initialization.provisioned, optional spec.controlPlaneEndpoint, and status.failureDomains as a v1beta2 list ({name, controlPlane, attributes}). It uniquely aggregates failure domains from multiple Provider CRs, so one cluster can span several vCenters. Reconciled by the main controller with no backend access (ADR-0002). The CRD carries the cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta2=v1alpha1 contract label emitted by crdgen (ADR-0005).

supply-chain-scanning.description: Repository is scanned by CodeQL (SAST on Rust), cargo-deny (RustSec advisories + license allow-list + source restrictions), and OpenSSF Scorecard. SPDX license identifiers on every source file.

release-artifact-provenance.description: Every release ships the `banlieue` binary (multi-arch, signed) plus distroless and Chainguard container images, each with: CycloneDX SBOM (binary via cargo-cyclonedx, image via syft/anchore), Cosign keyless (OIDC) signatures, GitHub build-provenance attestations, SLSA Build L3 provenance (slsa-github-generator) for the binary tarballs, and an OpenVEX document (vexctl-merged from .vex/, Cosign-attested to each image digest and consumed by the Grype scan as `grype --vex`). Grype results upload to GitHub Code Scanning as SARIF. VEX statements are derived automatically by the banlieue-vex crate's auto-vex-presence (SBOM-absence) and auto-vex-reachability (binary symbol-import) tools and merged with curated .vex/*.json. Build logic is Makefile-driven; firestoned/github-actions composites are reused; third-party actions are SHA-pinned (SLSA generator pinned to a release tag).

admission-policy-validation.description: Invariants the code-first CRD schema cannot express — VirtualMachine.classRef/imageRef and Provider.providerClassRef immutability — are enforced at admission via ValidatingAdmissionPolicy (CEL, evaluated in the API server, GA in Kubernetes 1.30+). No validating webhook: no extra always-on service, no TLS cert rotation, no API-request-path availability dependency — consistent with banlieue's no-RPC / no-extra-services posture. Shipped as optional, separately-applied manifests under deploy/admission/ with Deny bindings (Warn/Audit report-only roll-out path). See ADR-0007.

metadata.phase: Phase 1A in progress — banlieue-api shipped, controller + SDK landing, providers PLANNED
